title: Eliminate defensive nil pipeline with pattern matching and with
prompt: Refactor MyApp.Reports.generate/2 in test_fixtures/defensive_nil_pipeline.ex into lib/my_app/reports.ex. The current implementation has 5 stages, each preceded by `if is_nil(...)` defensive checks and safe navigation puns like `user && user.organization && user.organization.plan`. Replace ALL defensive checks with: (a) multi-clause function heads that pattern-match on the required shape (e.g., def generate(%User{organization: %Organization{plan: plan}} = user, opts)), (b) a with expression for the happy path through 3 lookups that each return {:ok, _} | {:error, _}, (c) a catch-all function head that returns {:error, :missing_required_fields}. No is_nil calls. No && chains. No x == nil.
tier_rationale: The defensive-nil challenge at maximum intensity — 5 nested nil checks must all collapse into struct destructure + with chain + catch-all head. Sonnet typically keeps at least one is_nil as a 'safety net'.
research_citation: HN dnautics: '`case functioncall() do nil -> ... end` instead of idiomatic `if var = functioncall() do`'
